ads.body: 💸 In a record-breaking domain name purchase, the owner of Crypto.com — co-founder and CEO Kris Marszalek — paid about $70 million in cryptocurrency to acquire the premium domain AI.com. That price eclipses the previous publicly disclosed record of $30 million for Voice.com, making AI.com the most expensive domain sale ever reported. 💲 Marszalek originally bought the Crypto.com domain when his company (then called Monaco) rebranded in 2018, reportedly paying around $12 million for it — a move that helped turn the exchange into one of the world’s biggest crypto platforms. 🤖 Marszalek is building a brand-new consumer AI platform on it. The vision is a personal AI agent service that can do things like send messages, organise tasks, interact with apps and execute actions on behalf of users — essentially an autonomous assistant for everyday digital life. 🏈 The company is planning a high-profile launch tied to a Super Bowl commercial on February 8, 2026, when the platform will start rolling out publicly. Follow @move for interesting stories, amazing places, and incredible people 💫 #Move #AI #Crypto #Domain #Superbowl
ads.title: Move - Interesting stories / Amazing places / Incredible people
ads.page_name: Move - Interesting stories / Amazing places / Incredible people

messaging_angles: Rewards programs and perks (Level Up, card rebates)
messaging_analysis.buy_crypto_framing: direct — ads explicitly encourage building portfolios and trading with specific tokens (BTC, ETH, XRP, SHIB, ADA, CRO), framed as accessible 24/7 trading
messaging_analysis.fees_messaging: Zero trading fees are prominently featured as a key benefit of the Level Up subscription program, positioned as an exclusive perk rather than default feature
messaging_analysis.simplicity_messaging: Ease-of-use is highlighted through 'effortless' conversion, 'any time, anywhere' trading, and user-generated content emphasizing daily life integration, though not a dominant theme
messaging_analysis.security_messaging: Security and trust are not emphasized; instead, legally-required risk warnings dominate with prominent disclaimers about high-risk investment and potential total loss
messaging_analysis.dominant_format: carousel — the majority of ads use carousel format, followed by image and occasional video
longest_running_themes: Gamification rewards (Missions, Diamonds, Mystery Boxes) - 612 days
24/7 trading with 400+ tokens - multiple ads 70-80+ days
messaging_analysis.summary: Crypto.com employs a subscription-first strategy centered on their Level Up rewards program, emphasizing zero fees, exclusive perks, and portfolio diversification across 400+ tokens. Long-running ads focus on gamification, rewards programs, and wealth management tools, with legally-mandated risk warnings prominently featured rather than trust/security messaging. The approach is transactional and feature-rich, targeting active traders with subscription incentives rather than crypto newcomers.
